hypothesis: In metal superhydrides at 150–300 GPa, a parent metal lattice interstitial electron localization function (ELF) maximum ≥ 0.70 predicts that the hydrogenated compound will have an electron–phonon coupling constant λ ≥ 1.2 and a superconducting Tc ≥ 120 K (µ* = 0.10), whereas parent lattices with ELF maxima < 0.70 will not meet both these thresholds.
